final_answer:
Implemented: the operator-uniqueness plan is now in code and validated with tests.

Why this matters: validator approval/voting can no longer be ambiguous when one operator appears across multiple validator IDs, so the `>2/3` flow stays deterministic and safe under churn.

What I changed:
1. Added shared operator-conflict enforcement for new approval proposals in [validator_lifecycle.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/keeper/validator_lifecycle.go).
2. Enforced that check in `ApproveValidator` (new proposal path) and `JoinValidator` (execution-time safety) in [validator_lifecycle.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/keeper/validator_lifecycle.go).
3. Hardened active-voter resolution to fail if one operator maps to multiple active validators in [validator_lifecycle.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/keeper/validator_lifecycle.go).
4. Added active-operator uniqueness validation in genesis state validation in [genesis.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/types/genesis.go).
5. Added defensive init-genesis uniqueness guard in [genesis.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/keeper/genesis.go).
6. Added regression tests for active-conflict, pending-conflict, join-conflict, and genesis duplicate-operator rejection in [msg_server_test.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/keeper/msg_server_test.go) and [genesis_test.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/types/genesis_test.go).

Checks run:
1. `cd bridge/gilt-consensus && go test ./x/stake/keeper -run 'TestKeeperTestSuite/TestNativeValidatorApproval|TestKeeperTestSuite/TestNativeValidatorJoinRejectsOperatorConflictWithActiveValidator' -count=1` passed.
2. `cd bridge/gilt-consensus && go test ./x/stake/types -run 'TestGenesisState_Validate' -count=1` passed.
3. `cd bridge/gilt-consensus && go test ./x/stake/... -count=1` passed.

Failure encountered and fixed:
1. One full-suite failure occurred in the new genesis test due validator-set order assumptions (index-based update caused duplicate `valID` in the test fixture).
2. Fixed by updating the target validator in the set by `valID` lookup, not by index, then reran full suite successfully.

What should be done next:
1. If you want, I can now apply the same operator-uniqueness rule to any other admission-related module paths outside `x/stake` (read-only audit first).
